title: Enabling the electrochemical simulation of Li-ion battery electrodes with anisotropic tortuosity in COMSOL Multiphysics‚ďá
author: Goel, Thornton

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: The existing implementation of the model equations in COMSOL can be accessed from the porous electrode sub-node in the Li-ion battery module by enabling the ‚ÄúEquation View‚ÄĚ option. Table¬†1 summarizes all the input values required by COMSOL for solving the equations pertaining to the ion-transport inside the electrolyte phase of a porous electrode. The other model equations can be found here [8].Table 1List of inputs that are required by COMSOL for simulating the ion-transport within the electrolyte phase of a porous electrode.Table 1VariableUnitsDefinitionFC/molFaraday's constantRJ/(mol K)Universal gas constantTKTemperature of the simulation‚ąąl‚ÄďVolume fraction of the electrolyte phaseŌÉl=[ŌÉl,xxŌÉl,xyŌÉl,xzŌÉl,yxŌÉl,yyŌÉl,yzŌÉl,zxŌÉl,zyŌÉl,zz]S/mIntrinsic electrolyte conductivity (tensor)Dlm¬≤/sIntrinsic electrolyte diffusivity (scalar)t+‚ÄďTransference number for Li-ions‚ąāln(f¬Ī)‚ąāln(cl)‚ÄďSalt activity dependence on the salt concentrationf(‚ąąl)‚ÄďRatio between the effective and intrinsic electrolyte properties (diffusivity and conductivity)

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: It can be noted from Table¬†1 that COMSOL allows for an ionic conductivity tensor ŌÉl to be input into the model, while only a scalar input value is allowed for the ionic diffusivity Dl. Since the ionic conductivity and diffusivity are related in both the diluted and concentrated binary electrolytes [12], this disparate treatment for these two quantities is unphysical. Therefore, one needs to be cautious while inputting these quantities into the model if applying the model beyond the intended 1D/pseudo-1D cases.

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Table¬†2 summarizes the implementation of all the ion-transport equations in a porous medium in COMSOL. The quantities cl and Ōēl represent the electrolyte concentration (mol/m3) and the electrolyte potential (V), respectively. The reaction source term is denoted by Rl, which is equal to itotal/F. The quantity itotal represents the total reaction current per unit volume of the porous media.Table 2Implementation of the ion-transport equations in COMSOL.Table 2Eq. No.EquationUnitsDescription3.ix=ilx+2RT(1+‚ąāln(f¬Ī)‚ąāln(cl))(1‚ąít+)f(‚ąąl)(ŌÉl,xx‚ąācl‚ąāx+ŌÉl,xy‚ąācl‚ąāy+ŌÉl,xz‚ąācl‚ąāz)F(max(cl,0.1))A/m¬≤X component of the electrolyte current density4.iy=ily+2RT(1+‚ąāln(f¬Ī)‚ąāln(cl))(1‚ąít+)f(‚ąąl)(ŌÉl,yx‚ąācl‚ąāx+ŌÉl,yy‚ąācl‚ąāy+ŌÉl,yz‚ąācl‚ąāz)F(max(cl,0.1))A/m¬≤Y component of the electrolyte current density5.iz=ilz+2RT(1+‚ąāln(f¬Ī)‚ąāln(cl))(1‚ąít+)f(‚ąąl)(ŌÉl,zx‚ąācl‚ąāx+ŌÉl,zy‚ąācl‚ąāy+ŌÉl,zz‚ąācl‚ąāz)F(max(cl,0.1))A/m¬≤Z component of the electrolyte current density6.ilx=‚ąíf(‚ąąl)(ŌÉl,xx‚ąāŌēl‚ąāx+ŌÉl,xy‚ąāŌēl‚ąāy+ŌÉl,xz‚ąāŌēl‚ąāz)A/m¬≤X component of the electrolyte current density due to migration7.ily=‚ąíf(‚ąąl)(ŌÉl,yx‚ąāŌēl‚ąāx+ŌÉl,yy‚ąāŌēl‚ąāy+ŌÉl,yz‚ąāŌēl‚ąāz)A/m¬≤Y component of the electrolyte current density due to migration8.ilz=‚ąíf(‚ąąl)(ŌÉl,zx‚ąāŌēl‚ąāx+ŌÉl,zy‚ąāŌēl‚ąāy+ŌÉl,zz‚ąāŌēl‚ąāz)A/m¬≤Z component of the electrolyte current density due to migration9.NLi+,x=‚ąíf(‚ąąl)Dl‚ąācl‚ąāx+ixt+Fmol/(m¬≤s)X component of the Li-ion flux in the electrolyte10.NLi+,y=‚ąíf(‚ąąl)Dl‚ąācl‚ąāy+iyt+Fmol/(m¬≤s)Y component of the Li-ion flux in the electrolyte11.NLi+,z=‚ąíf(‚ąąl)Dl‚ąācl‚ąāz+izt+Fmol/(m¬≤s)Z component of the Li-ion flux in the electrolyte12.NPF6‚ąí,x=‚ąíf(‚ąąl)Dl‚ąācl‚ąāx‚ąíix(1‚ąít+)Fmol/(m¬≤s)X component of the anion (PF6‚ąí) flux in the electrolyte13.NPF6‚ąí,y=‚ąíf(‚ąąl)Dl‚ąācl‚ąāy‚ąíiy(1‚ąít+)Fmol/(m¬≤s)Y component of the anion (PF6‚ąí) flux in the electrolyte14.NPF6‚ąí,z=‚ąíf(‚ąąl)Dl‚ąācl‚ąāz‚ąíiz(1‚ąít+)Fmol/(m¬≤s)Z component of the anion (PF6‚ąí) flux in the electrolyte15.‚ąąl‚ąācl‚ąāt=f(‚ąąl)(‚ąā‚ąāxDl‚ąācl‚ąāx+‚ąā‚ąāyDl‚ąācl‚ąāy+‚ąā‚ąāzDl‚ąācl‚ąāz)+Rl‚ąíitotalt+F‚ąí‚ąāt+‚ąāx.ix+‚ąāt+‚ąāy.iy+‚ąāt+‚ąāz.izFmol/(m3s)Mass conservation equation in the electrolyte

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: For a porous medium, the effective transport property of the medium is a function of the intrinsic value of the property, the phase volume fraction, and the tortuosity [13]. For instance, the effective electrode diffusivity Deff in a porous electrode is obtained as [13](1)Deff=‚ąąlŌĄ2Dl,where ŌĄ represents the electrode tortuosity. The effective electrolyte conductivity is calculated in the same manner by substituting the corresponding value of intrinsic conductivity in Eq.¬†(1). Furthermore, we refer to the ratio of the effective transport property to the corresponding intrinsic value as the correction factor,(2)f(‚ąąl)=DeffDl=‚ąąlŌĄ2.

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Thus, it can be seen that the correction factor is a function of the electrode tortuosity. Now for an electrode with anisotropic effective diffusivity (due to an anisotropy in the microstructure), the correction factor would have different values along different axes. As mentioned before, the current COMSOL implementation assumes the electrode to have isotropic tortuosity, and thus, it only uses one correction factor f(‚ąąl) to calculate the current densities and ion fluxes in all three directions (Eq. 3‚Äď14), and for defining the mass conservation equation (Eq. 15). To account for the effect of the anisotropic tortuosity, we modified the COMSOL implementation by replacing the single correction factor f(‚ąąl) in all the equations by the direction-specific correction factor fj(‚ąąl), where j=x,y,or z. The modified equations are listed in Table¬†3 along with a comparison with the existing equations. It should be noted that while doing so, one needs to select ‚ÄúNo correction‚ÄĚ in the ‚ÄúEffective Transport Parameter Correction‚ÄĚ sub-menu in the porous electrode sub-node, and then modify the ion-transport equations, as discussed above. The correction factors, fx(‚ąąl), fy(‚ąąl), and fz(‚ąąl) should be treated as parameters, and their value should be set in the parameter table under ‚ÄúGlobal Definitions.‚ÄĚTable 3Side by side comparison of the existing implementation of the ion-transport equations in COMSOL, and the modified implementation to simulate the anisotropic tortuosity case.Table 3Implementation in COMSOLModified Implementationix=ilx+2RT(1+‚ąāln(f¬Ī)‚ąāln(cl))(1‚ąít+)f(‚ąąl)(ŌÉl,xx‚ąācl‚ąāx+ŌÉl,xy‚ąācl‚ąāy+ŌÉl,xz‚ąācl‚ąāz)F(max(cl,0.1))ix=ilx+2RT(1+‚ąāln(f¬Ī)‚ąāln(cl))(1‚ąít+)fx(‚ąąl)(ŌÉl,xx‚ąācl‚ąāx)F(max(cl,0.1))iy=ily+2RT(1+‚ąāln(f¬Ī)‚ąāln(cl))(1‚ąít+)f(‚ąąl)(ŌÉl,yx‚ąācl‚ąāx+ŌÉl,yy‚ąācl‚ąāy+ŌÉl,yz‚ąācl‚ąāz)F(max(cl,0.1))iy=ily+2RT(1+‚ąāln(f¬Ī)‚ąāln(cl))(1‚ąít+)fy(‚ąąl)(ŌÉl,yy‚ąācl‚ąāy)F(max(cl,0.1))iz=ilz+2RT(1+‚ąāln(f¬Ī)‚ąāln(cl))(1‚ąít+)f(‚ąąl)(ŌÉl,zx‚ąācl‚ąāx+ŌÉl,zy‚ąācl‚ąāy+ŌÉl,zz‚ąācl‚ąāz)F(max(cl,0.1))iz=ilz+2RT(1+‚ąāln(f¬Ī)‚ąāln(cl))(1‚ąít+)fz(‚ąąl)(ŌÉl,zz‚ąācl‚ąāz)F(max(cl,0.1))ilx=‚ąíf(‚ąąl)(ŌÉl,xx‚ąāŌēl‚ąāx+ŌÉl,xy‚ąāŌēl‚ąāy+ŌÉl,xz‚ąāŌēl‚ąāz)ilx=‚ąífx(‚ąąl)(ŌÉl,xx‚ąāŌēl‚ąāx)ily=‚ąíf(‚ąąl)(ŌÉl,yx‚ąāŌēl‚ąāx+ŌÉl,yy‚ąāŌēl‚ąāy+ŌÉl,yz‚ąāŌēl‚ąāz)ily=‚ąífy(‚ąąl)(ŌÉl,yy‚ąāŌēl‚ąāy)ilz=‚ąíf(‚ąąl)(ŌÉl,zx‚ąāŌēl‚ąāx+ŌÉl,zy‚ąāŌēl‚ąāy+ŌÉl,zz‚ąāŌēl‚ąāz)ilz=‚ąífz(‚ąąl)(ŌÉl,zz‚ąāŌēl‚ąāz)NLi+,x=‚ąíf(‚ąąl)Dl‚ąācl‚ąāx+ixt+FNLi+,x=‚ąífx(‚ąąl)Dl‚ąācl‚ąāx+ixt+FNLi+,y=‚ąíf(‚ąąl)Dl‚ąācl‚ąāy+iyt+FNLi+,y=‚ąífy(‚ąąl)Dl‚ąācl‚ąāy+iyt+FNLi+,z=‚ąíf(‚ąąl)Dl‚ąācl‚ąāz+izt+FNLi+,z=‚ąífz(‚ąąl)Dl‚ąācl‚ąāz+izt+FNPF6‚ąí,x=‚ąíf(‚ąąl)Dl‚ąācl‚ąāx‚ąíix(1‚ąít+)FNPF6‚ąí,x=‚ąífx(‚ąąl)‚ąācl‚ąāx‚ąíix(1‚ąít+)FNPF6‚ąí,y=‚ąíf(‚ąąl)Dl‚ąācl‚ąāy‚ąíiy(1‚ąít+)FNPF6‚ąí,y=‚ąífy(‚ąąl)Dl‚ąācl‚ąāy‚ąíiy(1‚ąít+)FNPF6‚ąí,z=‚ąíf(‚ąąl)Dl‚ąācl‚ąāz‚ąíiz(1‚ąít+)FNPF6‚ąí,z=‚ąífz(‚ąąl)Dl‚ąācl‚ąāz‚ąíiz(1‚ąít+)F‚ąąl‚ąācl‚ąāt=f(‚ąąl)(‚ąā‚ąāxDl‚ąācl‚ąāx+‚ąā‚ąāyDl‚ąācl‚ąāy+‚ąā‚ąāzDl‚ąācl‚ąāz)+Rl‚ąíitotalt+F‚ąí‚ąāt+‚ąāx.ix+‚ąāt+‚ąāy.iy+‚ąāt+‚ąāz.izF‚ąąl‚ąācl‚ąāt=(fx(‚ąąl)‚ąā‚ąāxDl‚ąācl‚ąāx+fy(‚ąąl)‚ąā‚ąāyDl‚ąācl‚ąāy+fz(‚ąąl)‚ąā‚ąāzDl‚ąācl‚ąāz)+Rl‚ąíitotalt+F‚ąí‚ąāt+‚ąāx.ix+‚ąāt+‚ąāy.iy+‚ąāt+‚ąāz.izF

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: We note that the suggested modification assumes that the off-diagonal components in the ionic conductivity tensor (Entry 5, Table¬†1) are zero. Further modifications would be needed when the off-diagonal components are non-zero. Since it is assumed that the ionic conductivity tensor is diagonal, we removed all the off-diagonal terms from the current density equations (Eq. 3‚Äď8, Table¬†2), as shown in Table¬†3. Finally, we note that although the variables that represent the ion-fluxes (Eq. 9‚Äď14, Table¬†2) are not used in the mass conservation equation (Eq. 15, Table¬†2), their modification is required as the values for the ion-fluxes are stored for post-simulation analyses.

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: The equations for the electronic current density is can be modified in a similar manner, as shown in Table¬†4. The potential of the active material phase in the porous electrode is denoted by Ōēs. The isotropic correction factor is denoted by g(‚ąąs), where ‚ąąs is the volume fraction of the active material in a porous electrode. The anisotropic correction factors are denoted by gj(‚ąąs), where j=x,y,or z. The electronic conductivity of the active material is denoted by ŌÉs, which is treated as a tensor in COMSOL's current implementation. Therefore, as with ionic conductivity, the earlier discussion above about the applicability of the suggested modification for a diagonal tensor also holds here. It should be noted that no other model equation was modified in this work. We call the model with the modified equations as the modified battery model from hereon.Table 4Side by side comparison of the existing implementation of the electronic current density equations in COMSOL and the modified implementation to simulate the anisotropic tortuosity case.Table 4Implementation in COMSOLModified Implementationis,x=‚ąíg(‚ąąs)(ŌÉs,xx‚ąāŌēs‚ąāx+ŌÉs,xy‚ąāŌēs‚ąāy+ŌÉs,xz‚ąāŌēs‚ąāz)is,x=‚ąígx(‚ąąs)(ŌÉs,xx‚ąāŌēs‚ąāx)is,y=‚ąíg(‚ąąs)(ŌÉs,yx‚ąāŌēs‚ąāx+ŌÉs,yy‚ąāŌēs‚ąāy+ŌÉs,yz‚ąāŌēs‚ąāz)is,y=‚ąígy(‚ąąs)(ŌÉs,yy‚ąāŌēs‚ąāy)is,z=‚ąíg(‚ąąs)(ŌÉs,zx‚ąāŌēs‚ąāx+ŌÉs,zy‚ąāŌēs‚ąāy+ŌÉs,zz‚ąāŌēs‚ąāz)is,z=‚ąígz(‚ąąs)(ŌÉs,zz‚ąāŌēs‚ąāz)
